concerns: The Python spike script runs an interactive login flow, which cannot be executed automatically in CI environments without mock inputs.
The Pyrogram client is initialized with in_memory=True, meaning it will not persist the session state to disk, which limits testing of actual session re-use.
No dependency file (e.g., requirements.txt) was added to specify required library versions for the script.
The WebSocket API specification uses AsyncAPI version 3.0.0, which might have limited compatibility with some standard toolchains.
The openapi.yaml specification does not define security or authentication schemes (e.g., Bearer auth or cookies) for endpoints, which are essential for security.
DatabaseMigrationTest.java executes DELETE statements directly on the tables to clean them up, which could fail if the tables do not exist yet or have integrity constraint violations.
